lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Hello adventurer, welcome to the Entrana furnace.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Would you like me to explain my craft to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please. I'd be fascinated to hear what you do.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: I'm extremely pleased to hear that! I've always wanted an apprentice. Let me talk you through the secrets of glassblowing.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Glass is made from soda ash and silica. We get our soda ash by collecting seaweed from the rocks - the prevailing currents make the north-west corner of the island the best place to find it, it can also be found in

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: your nets sometimes when you're fishing, on Karamja island or at the Piscatoris Fishing Colony in the nets there. To turn seaweed into soda ash, all you need to do is burn it on a fire. Feel free to use the range in

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: my house for that; it's the one directly west of here. Next we collect sand from the sandpit that you'll also find just west of here, there are others located in Yanille and Shilo Village.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: You'll need a bucket to carry it in. Tell you what, you can have this old one of mine.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Bring the sand and the soda ash back here and melt them together in the furnace, and there you have it - molten glass!

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: There are many things you can use the molten glass for once you have made it. Depending on how talented you are, you could try turning it into something, like a fishbowl, for example. If you'd like to try your hand at

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: the fine art of glassblowing you can use my spare glassblowing pipe. I think I left it on the chest of drawers in my house this morning.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Alternatively I am always happy to buy the molten glass from you, saves me running about making it for myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds good. How much will you pay me?

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Tell you what, because you've been interested in my art, I'll pay you the premium price of 20 gold pieces for each piece of molten glass you bring me

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I doubt I'll ever turn my hand to glassblowing.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Ok, suit yourself. Nobody seems to be interested in the skilled crafts these days.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: If you should change your mind, come back and talk to me.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Ah [player name], have you come to sell me some molten glass?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Fantastic! Let's see, for that much glass I could pay you ...

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: ... [amount] gold pieces!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you mad? I've run all over the island to make this glass.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Pity. Well you can find me here if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, that sounds like a fair price.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Here you are then.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Thanks very much, [player name]. I'll buy any more that you bring.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Umm, not much point me trying to pay you for glass you don't have, is there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, actually, if you don't mind... question then.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: I'll make it simple for you. You bring glass, me pay shiny gold coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: ...errr, well should you get any I'm quite happy to pay for it. Remember, I'll pay you 20 gold pieces for each piece of molten glass you get for me.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: So what did you want to talk to me about then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me how to make molten glass again, please?

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Well, I am very busy at the moment, but I guess I can take 5 minutes off for my favourite student.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Glass is made from soda ash and silica. We get our soda ash by collecting seaweed from the rocks - the prevailing currents make the north-west corner of the island the best place to find it, it can also be found in

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: your nets sometimes when you're fishing, on Karamja island or at the Piscatoris Fishing Colony in the nets there. To turn seaweed into soda ash, all you need to do is burn it on a fire. Feel free to use the range in

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Fritz the Glassblower: my house for that; it's the one directly west of here. Next we collect sand from the sandpit that you'll also find just west of here, there are others located in Yanille and Shilo Village.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: You can use the bucket I gave you earlier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have that bucket with me. Could you give me another one, please?

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Do I look like I'm made of buckets? You'll just have to buy another from a general store.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just thought I'd drop in and say hello.

lines.speaker: Fritz the Glassblower
lines.text: Hello then. I'm afraid I don't have any time to chat though. The druids of Taverley have placed a huge order for glass vials, so I'm very busy.
